Sauti Kenya

Voice Of Change

Countries
1 +
Women Impacted
100

SAUTI Kenya is a leadership program under Ushindi Empowerment Group designed to strengthen the role of women and youth in advocacy. This initiative works towards building sustainable county-focused capacity of women led civic society actors and strengthening their organizational capacity. Our advocacy efforts are centered on four thematic areas i.e. Climate change, peace security and governance, poverty eradication and health. Our previous projects (Cohort 1 and 2) have focused on women led initiatives working on climate change and peace, security & governance.

OUR PROJECTS

Shelters and Helplines Cohort

Strengthening the Organizational Capacity and Sustainability of Women's Shelters and Helplines in Kenya

We work with communities and community leaders in four focus areas: Economic Empowerment, Gender Justice, The Creative Economy, and Climate Action. Through our work, we seek to improve livelihoods in order to guarantee economic empowerment, health and safety, reduce inequalities, improve education, and protect our environment.

Our Partners

Cohort 2: Strengthening the role of women in climate change mitigation

The main objective of the project was to work with Kenyan women and communities to address climate change by increasing awareness and use of clean domestic/energy in rural Kenya while also offering options for economic advancement and empowerment. The project identified sixty (60) women specifically women who run CBOs, Chama leaders, women in agriculture and farmer associations, female community influencers in the target counties with potential or engaged in part in effective advocacy efforts in the community and equipped them with skills on effective advocacy and entrepreneurship, specifically the making, use and sale of briquettes as a business for self-reliance by the end of the project. 600 women group members through were reached through cascade trainings by the trained leaders on clean energy for domestic use ensuring 2400 people access to clean energy through the use of briquettes. The project was led by US Kenya Exchange Alumni and was implemented in Kericho, Kakamega and Kisumu counties.

Cohort 1: Strengthening the role of women in peace and governance-Kisumu, Nairobi, Mombasa

This was an initiative that worked towards building sustainable county-focused capacity of women-led civic society actors, female student leaders, community influencers, and common interest groups that focus on peace and counter-radicalization for effective promotion of human rights and conflict management in Mombasa County, Kisumu County, and parts of Nakuru County, Kenya. The project was led by a team of women leaders (US embassy program alumni) and like-minded partners with a passion to effect change in Kenya’s governance. The project managed to train 60 grassroots women leaders in effective advocacy and campaigning, advocacy communications, social entrepreneurship & organizational sustainability, and theater for community engagement. As a result, hundreds of community members were impacted through peace focused communication and campaigns, short films and theatre.

Impact Stories

Brenda Owiny SRHR Advocate, Digital Marketing Specialist - Sauti Kenya Alumni

A journey of a thousand miles begins with a step and for me, Sauti Kenya's program for Women in grassroots leadership came in handy in my career journey, empowering me with skills in advocacy and film production. This has enabled me to use film in advocacy for sustainable development.

Jemimah Kutata Founder Mona Disabled Women Community Based Organization - Sauti Kenya Alumni

Sauti Kenya Program has enabled me to have a unique voice in peace, security and governance. It has empowered me in film industry am chosen to participate in an upcoming movie soon. NAJIVUNIA KUWA MKENYA

Francisca Mwanza Precise Consultants Kenya - Sauti Kenya Alumni

I am indeed grateful for the Sauti Kenya fellowship program which made me amplify my voice to highlight the plight of the youth in my community."

Hyral Matete Founder Angels of Sunset Kids Talent Academy- Sauti Kenya Alumni

I have always wanted to be a play director but was never confident enough to do that. Sauti Kenya gave me a chance to be an assistant director. Since then, the experience has been a life changer, and directing kids' play at my talent academy has been made easier.
